name: Image build and Push

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  release:
  pull_request:
  workflow_dispatch:

# Cancel in-progress runs for same workflow and branch/PR
concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.head_ref || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

permissions:
  contents: read
  id-token: write
  packages: write

jobs:
  build:
    name: build
    uses: ./.github/workflows/base.yml
    with:
      job-type: build

  lint:
    name: lint
    uses: ./.github/workflows/base.yml
    with:
      job-type: lint

  # Fast amd64-only build for PRs
  build-docker:
    name: build-docker
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    uses: ./.github/workflows/base.yml
    with:
      job-type: docker
      clickhouse-versions: '["25.1"]'
      platforms: linux/amd64
      push-image: true

  # Multi-arch build only for main branch and releases (slower but complete)
  build-docker-multiarch:
    name: build-docker-multiarch
    if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'
    uses: ./.github/workflows/base.yml
    with:
      job-type: docker
      clickhouse-versions: '["25.1"]'
      platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64
      push-image: true

  validate-docker:
    name: validate-docker
    uses: ./.github/workflows/base.yml
    with:
      job-type: docker
      clickhouse-versions: '["24.5"]'
      platforms: linux/amd64
      load-image: true
      test-container: true

file.content:
# wrangler.toml configuration for clickhouse-monitor
# Docs: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/wrangler/configuration/

name = "clickhouse-monitor"
main = ".open-next/worker.js"
compatibility_date = "2024-12-29"

# Minification (set to false for debugging, true for production)
minify = true

# Disable keep_names to fix "__name is not defined" error (appears as "e is not defined" when minified)
# This is required for libraries like next-themes that convert scripts to strings
# See: https://opennext.js.org/cloudflare/howtos/keep_names
keep_names = false

# Upload source maps for better error stack traces in Cloudflare dashboard
# See: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/observability/source-maps/
# Disabled for production to reduce bundle size - enable for debugging if needed
upload_source_maps = false

# Compatibility flags
# Enable Node.js API: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/compatibility-flags/#nodejs-compatibility-flag
compatibility_flags = ["nodejs_compat", "nodejs_compat_populate_process_env", "global_fetch_strictly_public"]

# Assets
[assets]
directory = ".open-next/assets"
binding = "ASSETS"

# Service binding for self-reference
[[services]]
binding = "WORKER_SELF_REFERENCE"
service = "clickhouse-monitor"

# R2 bucket for incremental cache
[[r2_buckets]]
binding = "NEXT_INC_CACHE_R2_BUCKET"
bucket_name = "clickhouse-monitoring-inc-cache"

# Observability
[observability]
enabled = true

# Smart Placement
# Docs: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/smart-placement/#smart-placement
[placement]
mode = "smart"

# Durable Objects for cache queue
[[durable_objects.bindings]]
name = "NEXT_CACHE_DO_QUEUE"
class_name = "DOQueueHandler"

# SQLite migrations for Durable Objects
[[migrations]]
tag = "v1"
new_sqlite_classes = ["DOQueueHandler"]

# D1 database for tag cache
[[d1_databases]]
binding = "NEXT_TAG_CACHE_D1"
database_name = "clickhouse-monitor"
database_id = "f289f509-c33b-4218-8602-7a19d5d567bc"
migrations_dir = "src/db/migrations"

# KV namespace for incremental cache
[[kv_namespaces]]
binding = "NEXT_INC_CACHE_KV"
id = "78782c1fcf4141d9b6f26f2046f50ceb"

# Environment Variables
#
# For local development: Set these in .dev.vars file (not committed to git)
# For production: Set these in Cloudflare Dashboard or wrangler secret
#
# Required variables:
# - CLICKHOUSE_HOST: Comma-separated list of ClickHouse host URLs
# - CLICKHOUSE_USER: Comma-separated list of usernames (or single value for all hosts)
# - CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD: Comma-separated list of passwords (or single value for all hosts)
#
# Optional variables:
# - CLICKHOUSE_NAME: Comma-separated list of custom names for hosts
# - CLICKHOUSE_MAX_EXECUTION_TIME: Query timeout in seconds (default: 60)
# - CLICKHOUSE_TZ: Timezone for ClickHouse queries
#
# Example .dev.vars:
# CLICKHOUSE_HOST=http://localhost:8123
# CLICKHOUSE_USER=default
# CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD=your-password

[vars]
# Maximum query execution time in seconds before ClickHouse terminates the query.
# Prevents long-running queries from consuming resources. Used in clickhouse-client.ts.
CLICKHOUSE_MAX_EXECUTION_TIME = "60"

# Flag to indicate running in Cloudflare Workers environment.
# When "1", uses @clickhouse/client-web instead of @clickhouse/client (Node.js).
# Required because Workers runtime doesn't support Node.js net/tls modules.
CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS = "1"

[vars]
# Maximum query execution time in seconds before ClickHouse terminates the query.
# Prevents long-running queries from consuming resources. Used in clickhouse-client.ts.
CLICKHOUSE_MAX_EXECUTION_TIME = "60"

# Flag to indicate running in Cloudflare Workers environment.
# When "1", uses @clickhouse/client-web instead of @clickhouse/client (Node.js).
# Required because Workers runtime doesn't support Node.js net/tls modules.
CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS = "1"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Preview environment for PR deployments
# Deployed by GitHub Actions on pull requests.
# Inherits all top-level settings; overrides listed below.
#
# One-time setup required:
#   wrangler secret put CLICKHOUSE_HOST --env preview
#   wrangler secret put CLICKHOUSE_USER --env preview
#   wrangler secret put CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD --env preview
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
[env.preview]
name = "clickhouse-monitor-preview"

# Self-reference must point to the preview worker
[[env.preview.services]]
binding = "WORKER_SELF_REFERENCE"
service = "clickhouse-monitor-preview"

# Reuse the same R2 bucket (read-heavy monitoring data)
[[env.preview.r2_buckets]]
binding = "NEXT_INC_CACHE_R2_BUCKET"
bucket_name = "clickhouse-monitoring-inc-cache"

# Reuse the same D1 database for tag cache
[[env.preview.d1_databases]]
binding = "NEXT_TAG_CACHE_D1"
database_name = "clickhouse-monitor"
database_id = "f289f509-c33b-4218-8602-7a19d5d567bc"
migrations_dir = "src/db/migrations"

# Reuse the same KV namespace for incremental cache
[[env.preview.kv_namespaces]]
binding = "NEXT_INC_CACHE_KV"
id = "78782c1fcf4141d9b6f26f2046f50ceb"

content.text: Now rewrite the workflow to handle both preview and production:

input.content:
name: Deploy to Cloudflare Workers

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]
  release:
    types: [published]
  workflow_dispatch:

# Cancel in-progress runs for same PR / branch
concurrency:
  group: cloudflare-${{ github.head_ref || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

permissions:
  contents: read
  deployments: write
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Preview: deploy on every PR so reviewers can test changes live
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  preview:
    name: preview
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Setup Bun
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2

      - name: Extract build metadata
        id: meta
        run: |
          GIT_SHA="${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}"
          echo "git_sha=${GIT_SHA:0:7}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "git_ref=pr-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
          echo "build_timestamp=${BUILD_TIMESTAMP}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Next.js Cache
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: .next/cache
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-nextjs-preview-${{ hashFiles('**/bun.lock') }}-${{ hashFiles('**.[jt]s', '**.[jt]sx') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-nextjs-preview-${{ hashFiles('**/bun.lock') }}-
            ${{ runner.os }}-nextjs-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install

      - name: Build for Cloudflare
        env:
          NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_SHA: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_sha }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_REF: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_ref }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_TIMESTAMP: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.build_timestamp }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_CI: "true"
        run: bun run cf:build

      - name: Deploy preview to Cloudflare Workers
        id: deploy
        uses: cloudflare/wrangler-action@v3
        with:
          apiToken: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN }}
          accountId: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID }}
          command: deploy --env preview --minify

      - name: Comment preview URL on PR
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const previewUrl = 'https://clickhouse-monitor-preview.duyet.workers.dev';
            const sha = '${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_sha }}';
            const timestamp = '${{ steps.meta.outputs.build_timestamp }}';
            const body = [
              '### ☁️ Cloudflare Preview Deployment',
              '',
              `| Property | Value |`,
              `|----------|-------|`,
              `| **Preview URL** | ${previewUrl} |`,
              `| **Commit** | \`${sha}\` |`,
              `| **Deployed at** | ${timestamp} |`,
              '',
              '> This preview is automatically updated on every push to this PR.',
            ].join('\n');

            // Find existing bot comment to update
            const { data: comments } = await github.rest.issues.listComments({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
            });
            const botComment = comments.find(
              (c) => c.body?.includes('Cloudflare Preview Deployment')
            );

            if (botComment) {
              await github.rest.issues.updateComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                comment_id: botComment.id,
                body,
              });
            } else {
              await github.rest.issues.createComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: context.issue.number,
                body,
              });
            }

  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Production: deploy on merge to main, releases, or manual trigger
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  production:
    name: production
    if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    outputs:
      git_sha: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_sha }}
      git_ref: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_ref }}
      build_timestamp: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.build_timestamp }}
      deployment_url: ${{ steps.deployment-details.outputs.url }}
      deployment_id: ${{ steps.deployment-details.outputs.id }}

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Setup Bun
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2

      - name: Extract build metadata
        id: meta
        run: |
          GIT_SHA="${{ github.sha }}"
          echo "git_sha=${GIT_SHA:0:7}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "git_ref=${{ github.ref_name }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
          echo "build_timestamp=${BUILD_TIMESTAMP}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "Git SHA: ${GIT_SHA:0:7}"
          echo "Git Ref: ${{ github.ref_name }}"
          echo "Build Time: ${BUILD_TIMESTAMP}"

      - name: Next.js Cache
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: .next/cache
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-nextjs-${{ hashFiles('**/bun.lock') }}-${{ hashFiles('**.[jt]s', '**.[jt]sx') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-nextjs-${{ hashFiles('**/bun.lock') }}-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install

      - name: Build for Cloudflare
        env:
          NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_SHA: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_sha }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_REF: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_ref }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_TIMESTAMP: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.build_timestamp }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_CI: "true"
        run: |
          echo "Building with metadata:"
          echo "  SHA: ${NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_SHA}"
          echo "  Ref: ${NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_REF}"
          echo "  Time: ${NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_TIMESTAMP}"
          bun run cf:build

      - name: Deploy to Cloudflare Workers
        id: deploy
        uses: cloudflare/wrangler-action@v3
        with:
          apiToken: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN }}
          accountId: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID }}
          command: deploy --minify

      - name: Extract deployment details
        id: deployment-details
        run: |
          WORKER_NAME=$(grep '^name = ' wrangler.toml | head -n 1 | awk -F'"' '{print $2}')
          WORKER_URL="https://${WORKER_NAME}.duyet.workers.dev"
          echo "url=${WORKER_URL}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

          VERSION_ID=$(wrangler deployments list --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.[0].id' || echo "")
          if [ -n "$VERSION_ID" ]; then
            echo "id=${VERSION_ID}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

          echo "Worker URL: ${WORKER_URL}"
          echo "Version ID: ${VERSION_ID}"

      - name: Deployment summary
        run: |
          echo "### 🚀 Cloudflare Workers Deployment" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "**Deployed clickhouse-monitor**" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "| Property | Value |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "|----------|-------|" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "| URL | ${{ steps.deployment-details.outputs.url }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          if [ -n "${{ steps.deployment-details.outputs.id }}" ]; then
            echo "| Version ID | \`${{ steps.deployment-details.outputs.id }}\` |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          fi
          echo "| Commit | \`${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_sha }}\` |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "| Branch/Tag | \`${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_ref }}\` |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "✅ Deployment completed successfully!" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

toolUseResult.content:
name: Deploy to Cloudflare Workers

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]
  release:
    types: [published]
  workflow_dispatch:

# Cancel in-progress runs for same PR / branch
concurrency:
  group: cloudflare-${{ github.head_ref || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

permissions:
  contents: read
  deployments: write
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Preview: deploy on every PR so reviewers can test changes live
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  preview:
    name: preview
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Setup Bun
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2

      - name: Extract build metadata
        id: meta
        run: |
          GIT_SHA="${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}"
          echo "git_sha=${GIT_SHA:0:7}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "git_ref=pr-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
          echo "build_timestamp=${BUILD_TIMESTAMP}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Next.js Cache
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: .next/cache
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-nextjs-preview-${{ hashFiles('**/bun.lock') }}-${{ hashFiles('**.[jt]s', '**.[jt]sx') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-nextjs-preview-${{ hashFiles('**/bun.lock') }}-
            ${{ runner.os }}-nextjs-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install

      - name: Build for Cloudflare
        env:
          NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_SHA: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_sha }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_REF: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_ref }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_TIMESTAMP: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.build_timestamp }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_CI: "true"
        run: bun run cf:build

      - name: Deploy preview to Cloudflare Workers
        id: deploy
        uses: cloudflare/wrangler-action@v3
        with:
          apiToken: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN }}
          accountId: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID }}
          command: deploy --env preview --minify

      - name: Comment preview URL on PR
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const previewUrl = 'https://clickhouse-monitor-preview.duyet.workers.dev';
            const sha = '${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_sha }}';
            const timestamp = '${{ steps.meta.outputs.build_timestamp }}';
            const body = [
              '### ☁️ Cloudflare Preview Deployment',
              '',
              `| Property | Value |`,
              `|----------|-------|`,
              `| **Preview URL** | ${previewUrl} |`,
              `| **Commit** | \`${sha}\` |`,
              `| **Deployed at** | ${timestamp} |`,
              '',
              '> This preview is automatically updated on every push to this PR.',
            ].join('\n');

            // Find existing bot comment to update
            const { data: comments } = await github.rest.issues.listComments({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
            });
            const botComment = comments.find(
              (c) => c.body?.includes('Cloudflare Preview Deployment')
            );

            if (botComment) {
              await github.rest.issues.updateComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                comment_id: botComment.id,
                body,
              });
            } else {
              await github.rest.issues.createComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: context.issue.number,
                body,
              });
            }

  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Production: deploy on merge to main, releases, or manual trigger
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  production:
    name: production
    if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    outputs:
      git_sha: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_sha }}
      git_ref: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_ref }}
      build_timestamp: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.build_timestamp }}
      deployment_url: ${{ steps.deployment-details.outputs.url }}
      deployment_id: ${{ steps.deployment-details.outputs.id }}

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Setup Bun
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2

      - name: Extract build metadata
        id: meta
        run: |
          GIT_SHA="${{ github.sha }}"
          echo "git_sha=${GIT_SHA:0:7}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "git_ref=${{ github.ref_name }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
          echo "build_timestamp=${BUILD_TIMESTAMP}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "Git SHA: ${GIT_SHA:0:7}"
          echo "Git Ref: ${{ github.ref_name }}"
          echo "Build Time: ${BUILD_TIMESTAMP}"

      - name: Next.js Cache
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: .next/cache
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-nextjs-${{ hashFiles('**/bun.lock') }}-${{ hashFiles('**.[jt]s', '**.[jt]sx') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-nextjs-${{ hashFiles('**/bun.lock') }}-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install

      - name: Build for Cloudflare
        env:
          NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_SHA: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_sha }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_REF: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_ref }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_TIMESTAMP: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.build_timestamp }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_CI: "true"
        run: |
          echo "Building with metadata:"
          echo "  SHA: ${NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_SHA}"
          echo "  Ref: ${NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_REF}"
          echo "  Time: ${NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_TIMESTAMP}"
          bun run cf:build

      - name: Deploy to Cloudflare Workers
        id: deploy
        uses: cloudflare/wrangler-action@v3
        with:
          apiToken: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN }}
          accountId: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID }}
          command: deploy --minify

      - name: Extract deployment details
        id: deployment-details
        run: |
          WORKER_NAME=$(grep '^name = ' wrangler.toml | head -n 1 | awk -F'"' '{print $2}')
          WORKER_URL="https://${WORKER_NAME}.duyet.workers.dev"
          echo "url=${WORKER_URL}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

          VERSION_ID=$(wrangler deployments list --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.[0].id' || echo "")
          if [ -n "$VERSION_ID" ]; then
            echo "id=${VERSION_ID}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

          echo "Worker URL: ${WORKER_URL}"
          echo "Version ID: ${VERSION_ID}"

      - name: Deployment summary
        run: |
          echo "### 🚀 Cloudflare Workers Deployment" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "**Deployed clickhouse-monitor**" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "| Property | Value |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "|----------|-------|" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "| URL | ${{ steps.deployment-details.outputs.url }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          if [ -n "${{ steps.deployment-details.outputs.id }}" ]; then
            echo "| Version ID | \`${{ steps.deployment-details.outputs.id }}\` |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          fi
          echo "| Commit | \`${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_sha }}\` |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "| Branch/Tag | \`${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_ref }}\` |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "✅ Deployment completed successfully!" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Deploy to Cloudflare Workers

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  release:
    types: [published]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read
  deployments: write

jobs:
  deploy-cloudflare:
    name: deploy-cloudflare
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    # Export environment variables for next job
    outputs:
      git_sha: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_sha }}
      git_ref: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_ref }}
      build_timestamp: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.build_timestamp }}
      deployment_url: ${{ steps.deploy.outputs.url }}
      deployment_id: ${{ steps.deploy.outputs.id }}

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Setup Bun
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2

      - name: Extract build metadata
        id: meta
        run: |
          # Short SHA (7 chars)
          GIT_SHA="${{ github.sha }}"
          echo "git_sha=${GIT_SHA:0:7}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

          # Branch or tag name
          echo "git_ref=${{ github.ref_name }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

          # Build timestamp (UTC)
          BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
          echo "build_timestamp=${BUILD_TIMESTAMP}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

          # Debug output
          echo "Git SHA: ${GIT_SHA:0:7}"
          echo "Git Ref: ${{ github.ref_name }}"
          echo "Build Time: ${BUILD_TIMESTAMP}"

      - name: Next.js Cache
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: .next/cache
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-nextjs-${{ hashFiles('**/bun.lock') }}-${{ hashFiles('**.[jt]s', '**.[jt]sx') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-nextjs-${{ hashFiles('**/bun.lock') }}-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install

      - name: Build for Cloudflare
        env:
          # Build-time environment variables for About page
          NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_SHA: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_sha }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_REF: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_ref }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_TIMESTAMP: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.build_timestamp }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_CI: "true"
        run: |
          echo "Building with metadata:"
          echo "  SHA: ${NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_SHA}"
          echo "  Ref: ${NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_REF}"
          echo "  Time: ${NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_TIMESTAMP}"
          bun run cf:build

      - name: Deploy to Cloudflare Workers
        id: deploy
        uses: cloudflare/wrangler-action@v3
        with:
          apiToken: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN }}
          accountId: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID }}
          command: deploy --minify

      - name: Extract deployment details
        id: deployment-details
        run: |
          # Get worker URL from wrangler.toml (TOML format: name = "value")
          WORKER_NAME=$(grep '^name = ' wrangler.toml | head -n 1 | awk -F'"' '{print $2}')
          WORKER_URL="https://${WORKER_NAME}.duyet.workers.dev"

          # Extract version ID from deployment output (captured in wrangler action output)
          # The wrangler action outputs deployment details we can use
          echo "url=${WORKER_URL}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

          # Try to get version ID from recent deployment via wrangler
          VERSION_ID=$(wrangler deployments list --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.[0].id' || echo "")

          if [ -n "$VERSION_ID" ]; then
            echo "id=${VERSION_ID}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

          echo "Worker URL: ${WORKER_URL}"
          echo "Version ID: ${VERSION_ID}"

      - name: Deployment summary
        run: |
          echo "### 🚀 Cloudflare Workers Deployment" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "**Deployed clickhouse-monitor**" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "| Property | Value |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "|----------|-------|" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "| URL | ${{ steps.deployment-details.outputs.url }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          if [ -n "${{ steps.deployment-details.outputs.id }}" ]; then
            echo "| Version ID | \`${{ steps.deployment-details.outputs.id }}\` |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          fi
          echo "| Commit | \`${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_sha }}\` |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "| Branch/Tag | \`${{ steps.meta.outputs.git_ref }}\` |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "✅ Deployment completed successfully!" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

content.text:
All checks passed and pushed. Here's what was set up:

**Workflow changes (`cloudflare.yml`):**
- **PR preview**: Triggers on `pull_request` (opened/synchronize/reopened), deploys to `clickhouse-monitor-preview` worker via `wrangler deploy --env preview`, and auto-comments the preview URL on the PR (updates existing comment on subsequent pushes)
- **Production**: Triggers on push to `main`, releases, and manual dispatch — deploys to `clickhouse-monitor` worker (unchanged behavior)
- Added concurrency group to cancel stale PR deployments

**Wrangler config (`wrangler.toml`):**
- Added `[env.preview]` environment that deploys as `clickhouse-monitor-preview`
- Shares the same D1, KV, and R2 bindings with production (safe for a read-only monitoring dashboard)
- Service self-reference correctly points to the preview worker name

**One-time setup required** before preview deploys will work:
```bash
wrangler secret put CLICKHOUSE_HOST --env preview
wrangler secret put CLICKHOUSE_USER --env preview
wrangler secret put CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD --env preview
```
